samples.xml_description:
Converts 8-bit data to 16-bit data. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Input signal indicating valid data. 8-bit input data. Output signal indicating valid data. 16-bit output data. Parameter Description:
N/A type variables:
data_lock: 8-bit register to store the first valid input data.
flag: 1-bit flag to indicate when to store the first valid input data.
1. On the rising edge of the clock or the falling edge of the reset signal, if the reset signal is active, the flag is set to 0.
2. On the rising edge of the clock or the falling edge of the reset signal, if the reset signal is active, the data_lock register is cleared to 0.
3. On the rising edge of the clock, if valid_in is 1 and flag is 0, the first valid input data is stored in the data_lock register, and flag is set to 1.
4. On the rising edge of the clock, if valid_in is 1 and flag is 1, the second valid input data is concatenated with the data_lock register to produce the 16-bit output data (data_out), and flag is set to 0.

1. On the rising edge of the clock or the falling edge of the reset signal, if the reset signal is active, the flag is set to 0.

2. On the rising edge of the clock or the falling edge of the reset signal, if the reset signal is active, the data_lock register is cleared to 0.

3. On the rising edge of the clock, if valid_in is 1 and flag is 0, the first valid input data is stored in the data_lock register, and flag is set to 1.

4. On the rising edge of the clock, if valid_in is 1 and flag is 1, the second valid input data is concatenated with the data_lock register to produce the 16-bit output data (data_out), and flag is set to 0.
